Name: Bellsprout
Flavor-Text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although the leaves on its head are bitter enough to cause dizziness, they provide relief from weariness—even more so when boiled.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: No matter how much time and money is spent raising it, its flowers are the most beautiful when they bloom in the wild.

Name: Phantump
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died in the forest. Their cries sound like eerie screams.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 30 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to devour anyone daring to ravage the forest. To the creatures dwelling in the forest, it offers great kindness.

Name: Wimpod
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

Name: Golisopod
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: With a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater—or even air—right in two.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can’t use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Being consumed in Chandelure’s flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic [R] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It beats its wings together to create fire. As it moves in the steps of its beautiful dance, it bathes opponents in intense flames.

Name: Salandit
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

Name: Salazzle
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may leave your opponent’s Active Pokémon Burned and Poisoned.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point.

Name: Turtonator-GX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Attach 5 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Sandshrew
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives on snowy mountains. Its steel shell is very hard—so much so, it can’t roll its body up into a ball.

Name: Alolan Sandslash
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fleeing a volcanic eruption, it settled on a snowy mountain. As it races through the snowfields, it sends up a spray of snow.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

Name: Alolan Ninetales-GX
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tentacool
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t be healed during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They can be found lying dehydrated on beaches, but they are often still alive. When soaked in water, they will revive.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Normally, it has 80 poisonous tentacles. The longer one has been alive, the fewer tentacles it will have.

Name: Politoed
Effects: Search your deck for a Poliwag, a Poliwhirl, and a Poliwrath, and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: They gather on moonlit nights to form a large chorus. Their cries sound angry and not at all pleasant, but they are certainly distinctive.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Although it naturally prefers colder locales, Delibird in Alola seem able to withstand the heat to a certain extent.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: Each school has its own territory. Any intruders are mercilessly attacked with fangs bared.

Name: Sharpedo
Flavor-Text: It pursues its prey at speeds of 75 mph and finishes them off with fangs that can crush iron. It is known as the bully of the sea.

Name: Wailmer
Flavor-Text: It loves to startle people. It fills itself up with seawater and plays by bouncing around like a ball.

Name: Wailord
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Heal 30 damage from each of your [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Wailord pursue their prey in pods. With their large mouths, they can swallow entire schools of Wishiwashi whole.

Name: Snorunt
Flavor-Text: Their numbers seem to have rapidly increased in Alola. Custom has it that houses where Snorunt live will be prosperous for generations to come.

Name: Glalie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Legend says a boulder on an icy mountain absorbed the distress and regrets of a stranded mountaineer, giving rise to Glalie.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Born of an icicle, this Pokémon uses its frosty breath to make ice crystals, causing snow to fall.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hot days cause its body to melt. It can be restored by refreezing it, but the process leaves its body slightly warped.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may discard 2 [W] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Each of its two heads has a brain, and when they are in agreement, it attacks its enemies by exhaling a violent blizzard.

Name: Alomomola
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon from either player’s discard pile onto its owner’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: It uses its special mucus to close the wounds of injured Pokémon. The reason for this behavior remains unknown.

Name: Wishiwashi
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with a Wishiwashi-GX in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When it’s in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

Name: Wishiwashi-GX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mareanie
Effects: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

Name: Alolan Geodude
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attaches firmly to the portions of its body that are particularly magnetic.

Name: Alolan Graveler
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its preferred food is dravite. After it has eaten this mineral, crystals form inside the Pokémon, rising to the surface of part of its body.

Name: Alolan Golem
Effects: Flip a coin for each [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
This attack does 30 less damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It fires rocks charged with electricity. Even if the rock isn’t fired that accurately, just grazing an opponent will cause numbness and fainting.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Vikavolt-GX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack does 60 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Pom-Pom Punch attack’s base damage is 100 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio has sipped bright yellow nectar. Its bright, cheerful dance melts the hearts of its enemies.

Name: Tapu Koko-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may move any number of [L] Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its long tail often breaks off. It doesn’t really feel any pain, though, and the tail grows back, so Slowpoke isn’t particularly bothered.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon is Burned or Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spaces out while gazing at the sea. With Shellder’s poison flowing through its body, it becomes even spacier.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Unsanitary places are what they like best. They can be spotted in Alola, often with Grimer in hot pursuit.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Item card in your opponent’s discard pile.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Beware the poisonous liquid it shoots from its right arm. If even a little of it gets on you, you’ll experience the effects of the unidentified toxin.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

Name: Gothitelle
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio relaxes by swaying gently. This increases its psychic energy, which it then fires at its enemies.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: For each Pokémon in your opponent’s discard pile, put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
If there is no Stadium card in play, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. Its elegant, attractive dance will send the minds and hearts of its enemies to another world.

Name: Toxapex-GX
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 10 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
If your opponent’s Pokémon used an attack that isn’t a GX attack during their last turn, use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Its actual appearance is unknown. A scholar who saw what was under its rag was overwhelmed by horror and died of the shock.

Name: Dhelmise
Ability-Effect: Your [M] Pokémon’s attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Swinging its massive anchor, it can KO Wailord in a single blow. What appears to be green seaweed is actually its body.

Name: Tapu Lele-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lunala
Effects: This attack does 40 damage times the amount of [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be a female evolution of Cosmog. When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world.

Name: Machop
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It loves working out. As it gazes at its muscles, which continue to swell day by day, it becomes more and more dedicated to its training.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: It loves working out. As it gazes at its muscles, which continue to swell day by day, it becomes more and more dedicated to its training.

Name: Machoke
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks. Your opponent’s attacks and Abilities can’t place damage counters on your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As a result of its continual workouts, it has developed tremendous power. It uses that power to help people with their work.

Name: Machamp
Effects: This attack does 80 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took on their last turn.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It unleashes megaton-level punches that send opponents flying clear over the horizon.

Name: Sudowoodo
Ability-Effect: Your opponent can’t have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If they have 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Flavor-Text: To avoid attack, it mimics a tree. It will run off if splashed with water, which it hates.

Name: Gligar
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Nosepass
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The magnet in Nosepass’s nose provides an unerring compass, making it an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.

Name: Barboach
Flavor-Text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. This attack does 100 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: A glutton that devours anything that moves, it quietly lurks at the bottom of swamps, lying in wait for prey.

Name: Pancham
Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It grows up imitating the behavior of Pangoro, which it looks up to as a leader.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It’s considered to be a good Pokémon for beginners because of its friendliness, but its disposition grows rougher as it grows up.

Name: Lycanroc-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mudbray
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The mud stuck to Mudbray’s hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.

Name: Mudsdale
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

Name: Minior
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: It eats dust in the atmosphere. The composition of the dust determines the color of its core.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They awaken at dusk and take wing in the twilight, leading to the expression, “Get home before the Murkrow fly.”

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A single cry from this nocturnal Pokémon, and more than a hundred of its Murkrow cronies will assemble.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: It’s a fiend for gemstones, so it stalks Carbink. Unfortunately, Gabite almost always grabs them first.

Name: Absol
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of either player’s deck and put them back in any order.
Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Long ago, superstitions were spread about it, saying it brought disaster. This fed a hatred of it, and it was driven deep into the mountains.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It boasts superb physical strength. Those who wish to become Pangoro’s Trainer have no choice but to converse with their fists.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Discard a [M] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cells are all magnets. It uses magnetism to communicate with others of its kind.

Name: Metang
Effects: Discard a [M] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When two Beldum link together, their psychic power is doubled. Their intelligence, however, remains unchanged.

Name: Metagross-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [P] or [M] Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Giga Hammer during your next turn.
Search your deck for up to 5 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances become unusable.

Name: Solgaleo
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Fangs of the Sunne during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live in another world. The intense light it radiates from the surface of its body can make the darkest of nights light up like midday.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its adorable behavior and appearance make it popular with men and women, young and old. Its numbers are few, however.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They don’t like to reveal themselves in front of people. They live quietly in packs deep in the mountains.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it finds others of its kind, they all stick together. When enough of them have collected, the mass resembles a cumulonimbus cloud.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 2 more Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: It rides on the wind and slips into people’s homes. After it has turned a room into a cotton-filled mess, it giggles to itself and takes off.

Name: Sylveon-GX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to them into your opponent’s hand. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Comfey
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [Y] Energy attached to it can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent draws a card.
Flavor-Text: It attaches flowers to its highly nutritious vine. This revitalizes the flowers, and they give off an aromatic scent.

Name: Goomy
Effects: Flip a coin. If head, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The weakest of all Dragon-type Pokémon, it’s unable to breathe if its skin dries out, so it sticks to shady places.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Sliggoo and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has trouble drawing a line between friends and food. It will calmly try to melt and eat even those it gets along well with.

Name: Goodra
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears.

Name: Drampa
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a compassionate personality, but if it is angered, it will completely destroy its surroundings with its intense breath.

Name: Jangmo-o
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

Name: Hakamo-o
Flavor-Text: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

Name: Kommo-o-GX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
(You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eggs laid by Chansey are rich in nutrients and a favorite food of many Pokémon.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Even the most ferocious Pokémon become calm when they eat Blissey’s egg, which is said to be filled with happiness.

Name: Taillow
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

Name: Swellow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon used Agility during your last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If its two tail feathers are standing at attention, it is proof of good health. It soars elegantly in the sky.

Name: Castform
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Stadium cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It changes its form depending on the weather. Changes in the temperature or humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

Name: Rayquaza
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Look at the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Look at the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck, discard 1 of them, and put the other card back.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This amiable Pokémon is easy to train. But when battle is joined, it shows its ferocious side.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: From its beak, it fires embers at its prey. Once it has caught them, it grills them at high heat before feasting upon them.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its favorite foods are Wingull and Pikipek. It attacks with a powerful kick and grasps them firmly in its talons.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

Name: Bewear
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

Name: Komala
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever you attach an Energy from your hand to it, it is now Asleep.
Effects: This attack can be used if this Pokémon is Asleep. If it is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is born asleep, and it dies asleep. All its movements are apparently no more than the results of it tossing and turning in its dreams.

Name: Drampa-GX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 70 more damage.
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 10 cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aether Paradise Conservation Area
Description: Basic [G] and Basic [L] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) take 30 less damage from the opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Altar of the Moone
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) that has any [P] or [D] Energy attached to it is [C][C] less.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Altar of the Sunne
Description: [R] Pokémon and [M] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Weakness.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Aqua Patch
Description: Attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Brooklet Hill
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic [W] Pokémon or Basic [F] Pokémon, put it onto their Bench, and shuffle their deck.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Choice Band
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon-GX or Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Energy Loto
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Energy card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Recycler
Description: Shuffle 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Enhanced Hammer
Description: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Field Blower
Description: Choose up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards and Stadium cards in play (yours or your opponent’s) and discard them.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hala
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. If you have used your GX attack, draw 7 cards. If not, draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Mallow
Description: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, and put those cards on top of your deck in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Max Potion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Multi Switch
Description: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rescue Stretcher
Description: Choose 1:
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Turtonator-GX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Attach 5 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Ninetales-GX
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wishiwashi-GX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vikavolt-GX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack does 60 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Koko-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may move any number of [L] Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxapex-GX
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 10 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Lele-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lycanroc-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Metagross-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [P] or [M] Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Giga Hammer during your next turn.
Search your deck for up to 5 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sylveon-GX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to them into your opponent’s hand. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.

Name: Double Colorless Energy
Description: Double Colorless Energy provides [C][C] Energy.

Name: Alolan Sandshrew
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives on snowy mountains. Its steel shell is very hard—so much so, it can’t roll its body up into a ball.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Item card in your opponent’s discard pile.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Discard a [M] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cells are all magnets. It uses magnetism to communicate with others of its kind.
